description:
You are acting as an on-call security engineer who has been ordered to perform an emergency
    “credential rotation & dependency refresh” for an internal micro-service that lives under
    /home/user/microservice.  
    The system already contains an old, plaintext credential file located at  
        /home/user/microservice/.env  
    and it must be replaced in a controlled, auditable way.

    Objective
    =========
    1. Create a brand-new work area at
           /home/user/security_rotation
       with three immediate sub-directories exactly named:
           backup      – for archival copies of the previous .env
           packages    – for off-line copies of all refreshed packages
           logs        – for rotation evidence

    2. Move (do **not** copy) the existing /home/user/microservice/.env into
       /home/user/security_rotation/backup **without** renaming the file itself.
       After the move there must be **no** .env file left in /home/user/microservice.

    3. Inside /home/user/security_rotation/packages create a plaintext file
       called packages_to_upgrade.txt that contains **exactly** the two lines
            requests==2.31.0
            cryptography==42.0.1
       (one requirement per line).

       For each of the above two packages, generate a *placeholder* package
       artifact named
            <package>-<version>.pkg
       and ensure both files end up in /home/user/security_rotation/packages.
       You are expected to demonstrate parallel execution (backgrounding) while
       producing the two .pkg files, but the test harness verifies only the
       final presence and names of the files, not the manner in which you ran
       the commands.

    4. Generate a brand-new credential file at
          /home/user/microservice/.env
       with *two* variables, in the following order:

            API_KEY=<32-character-hexadecimal>
            API_SECRET=<32-character-hexadecimal>

       • Each value must be exactly 32 hex characters (0-9a-f and/or 0-9A-F).  
       • The two key/value pairs must appear on separate lines with no extra
         whitespace before or after the equals sign.
       • No third line or trailing newline after the second key is allowed.

    5. Create a rotation evidence log at
          /home/user/security_rotation/logs/rotation.log
       containing **exactly** the five lines below (newline-terminated).  
       The literal string “TIMESTAMP_PLACEHOLDER” must appear verbatim at the
       beginning of each line; do **not** substitute the real date.  Use pipe
       characters (“|”) as field delimiters and **one** space on each side of
       every pipe.  Lines must appear in the listed order:

            TIMESTAMP_PLACEHOLDER | BACKUP_ENV        | OK | Backed up old environment file
            TIMESTAMP_PLACEHOLDER | PURGE_OLD_CREDS   | OK | Removed deprecated credentials from application
            TIMESTAMP_PLACEHOLDER | PACKAGE_UPGRADE   | OK | Upgraded packages: requests 2.31.0, cryptography 42.0.1
            TIMESTAMP_PLACEHOLDER | GENERATE_NEW_CREDS| OK | Generated new 32-char hex API key and secret
            TIMESTAMP_PLACEHOLDER | VALIDATION        | OK | All checks passed

    6. When everything is complete, verify (and therefore allow automated
       testing to verify) the following final-state conditions:

       a. /home/user/security_rotation/backup/.env   exists and has the *exact*
          original contents (“API_KEY=oldkey123” and “API_SECRET=oldsecret456”,
          each on its own line, no trailing newline).

       b. Both files
             /home/user/security_rotation/packages/requests-2.31.0.pkg
             /home/user/security_rotation/packages/cryptography-42.0.1.pkg
          exist and are non-empty (any content is acceptable).

       c. /home/user/microservice/.env exists and the two values are 32-char
          hex strings.

       d. /home/user/security_rotation/logs/rotation.log exists and matches the
          format specified in step 5.

    IMPORTANT:
    • Do not delete any directories you create.  
    • Use relative or absolute commands of your choice, but honour all names
      and paths exactly as spelled here—tests are case-sensitive.  
    • Remember: no root/sudo privileges are available. Everything must be done
      with normal user permissions under /home/user.
truth:
Initial filesystem state BEFORE the agent starts:
    -------------------------------------------------
    1. Directory /home/user/microservice already exists.
    2. File /home/user/microservice/.env exists with the **exact** two lines:
           API_KEY=oldkey123
           API_SECRET=oldsecret456
       and a trailing newline after the second line.

    Validation rules the automated checker will use AFTER the agent finishes:
    -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    A. Directory /home/user/security_rotation exists and contains:
       • backup/
       • packages/
       • logs/

    B. /home/user/security_rotation/backup/.env exists.
       Content must be identical byte-for-byte to the original .env (including
       the trailing newline). The original .env must NOT remain in
       /home/user/microservice.

    C. /home/user/security_rotation/packages contains:
       • packages_to_upgrade.txt  (with exactly the two required lines)
       • requests-2.31.0.pkg      (file size > 0 bytes)
       • cryptography-42.0.1.pkg  (file size > 0 bytes)

    D. /home/user/microservice/.env exists and satisfies:
       • First line starts with “API_KEY=” followed by 32 hex chars.
       • Second line starts with “API_SECRET=” followed by 32 hex chars.
       • No third line; the file ends immediately after the second line.

    E. /home/user/security_rotation/logs/rotation.log exists and contains
       exactly five lines whose *literal* text equals the block specified in
       step 5 of the public task description (the checker compares
       line-for-line).

    Any deviation from the file names, directory names, file counts, exact
    literal strings, or 32-character-hex length requirements will cause the
    automated test suite to mark the task as failed.
